content.text: Create an Agent with subagent_type "statusline-setup" and the prompt "Configure my statusLine from my shell PS1 configuration"

input.prompt: Configure my statusLine from my shell PS1 configuration

data.prompt: Configure my statusLine from my shell PS1 configuration

1â†’# Enable Powerlevel10k instant prompt. Should stay close to the top of ~/.zshrc.
     2â†’# Initialization code that may require console input (password prompts, [y/n]

    98â†’ZSH_AUTOSUGGEST_MANUAL_REBIND=1
    99â†’
   100â†’# Customize the style that the suggestions are shown with.

   121â†’ZIM_HOME=${ZDOTDIR:-${HOME}}/.zim
   122â†’# Download zimfw plugin manager if missing.
   123â†’if [[ ! -e ${ZIM_HOME}/zimfw.zsh ]]; then
   124â†’  if (( ${+commands[curl]} )); then
   125â†’    curl -fsSL --create-dirs -o ${ZIM_HOME}/zimfw.zsh \
   126â†’        https://github.com/zimfw/zimfw/releases/latest/download/zimfw.zsh
   127â†’  else
   128â†’    mkdir -p ${ZIM_HOME} && wget -nv -O ${ZIM_HOME}/zimfw.zsh \
   129â†’        https://github.com/zimfw/zimfw/releases/latest/download/zimfw.zsh
   130â†’  fi
   131â†’fi
   132â†’# Install missing modules, and update ${ZIM_HOME}/init.zsh if missing or outdated.
   133â†’if [[ ! ${ZIM_HOME}/init.zsh -nt ${ZDOTDIR:-${HOME}}/.zimrc ]]; then
   134â†’  source ${ZIM_HOME}/zimfw.zsh init -q
   135â†’fi
   136â†’# Initialize modules.
   137â†’source ${ZIM_HOME}/init.zsh
   138â†’
   139â†’# ------------------------------
   140â†’# Post-init module configuration
   141â†’# ------------------------------
   142â†’
   143â†’#
   144â†’# zsh-history-substring-search
   145â†’#
   146â†’
   147â†’zmodload -F zsh/terminfo +p:terminfo
   148â†’# Bind ^[[A/^[[B manually so up/down works both before and after zle-line-init
   149â†’for key ('^[[A' '^P' ${terminfo[kcuu1]}) bindkey ${key} history-substring-search-up
   150â†’for key ('^[[B' '^N' ${terminfo[kcud1]}) bindkey ${key} history-substring-search-down
   151â†’for key ('k') bindkey -M vicmd ${key} history-substring-search-up
   152â†’for key ('j') bindkey -M vicmd ${key} history-substring-search-down
   153â†’unset key
   154â†’# }}} End configuration added by Zim install

1â†’#!/bin/sh
     2â†’# Claude Code status line - mirrors Powerlevel10k p10k prompt style
     3â†’# Shows: user@host  dir  git-branch  model  context%
     4â†’
     5â†’input=$(cat)
     6â†’
     7â†’cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd')
     8â†’model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name')
     9â†’remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
    10â†’
    11â†’# Shorten home directory to ~
    12â†’home="$HOME"
    13â†’short_cwd=$(echo "$cwd" | sed "s|^$home|~|")
    14â†’
    15â†’# Git branch (skip optional locks to avoid blocking)
    16â†’git_branch=""
    17â†’if git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    18â†’  branch=$(git -C "$cwd" -c core.fsmonitor=false symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null \
    19â†’           || git -C "$cwd" -c core.fsmonitor=false rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
    20â†’  if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
    21â†’    git_branch=" \033[0;33m $branch\033[0m"
    22â†’  fi
    23â†’fi
    24â†’
    25â†’# Context percentage with progress bar
    26â†’ctx_part=""
    27â†’if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
    28â†’  remaining_int=$(printf "%.0f" "$remaining")
    29â†’  used_int=$((100 - remaining_int))
    30â†’  if [ "$remaining_int" -le 20 ]; then
    31â†’    ctx_color="\033[0;31m"   # red when low
    32â†’  elif [ "$remaining_int" -le 40 ]; then
    33â†’    ctx_color="\033[0;33m"   # yellow when medium
    34â†’  else
    35â†’    ctx_color="\033[0;32m"   # green when plenty
    36â†’  fi
    37â†’  # 10-char bar: filled blocks for used, dashes for remaining
    38â†’  filled=$((used_int / 10))
    39â†’  empty=$((10 - filled))
    40â†’  bar=""
    41â†’  i=0
    42â†’  while [ $i -lt $filled ]; do
    43â†’    bar="${bar}#"
    44â†’    i=$((i + 1))
    45â†’  done
    46â†’  i=0
    47â†’  while [ $i -lt $empty ]; do
    48â†’    bar="${bar}-"
    49â†’    i=$((i + 1))
    50â†’  done
    51â†’  ctx_part=" ${ctx_color}[${bar}] ${used_int}%\033[0m"
    52â†’fi
    53â†’
    54â†’printf "\033[0;36m$(whoami)@$(hostname -s)\033[0m  \033[0;34m${short_cwd}\033[0m${git_branch}  \033[0;35m${model}\033[0m${ctx_part}"
    55â†’

    19â†’'builtin' 'setopt' 'no_aliases' 'no_sh_glob' 'brace_expand'
    20â†’
    21â†’() {
    22â†’  emulate -L zsh -o extended_glob
    23â†’
    24â†’  # Unset all configuration options. This allows you to apply configuration changes without

    31â†’  # The list of segments shown on the left. Fill it with the most important segments.
    32â†’  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_LEFT_PROMPT_ELEMENTS=(
    33â†’    # =========================[ Line #1 ]=========================
    34â†’    # os_icon               # os identifier
    35â†’    dir                     # current directory
    36â†’    vcs                     # git status
    37â†’    # =========================[ Line #2 ]=========================

    43â†’  # Right prompt on the last prompt line (where you are typing your commands) gets

    45â†’  # last prompt line gets hidden if it would overlap with left prompt.

    48â†’    status                  # exit code of the last command
    49â†’    command_execution_time  # duration of the last command
    50â†’    background_jobs         # presence of background jobs
    51â†’    direnv                  # direnv status (https://direnv.net/)
    52â†’    asdf                    # asdf version manager (https://github.com/asdf-vm/asdf)

    54â†’    anaconda                # conda environment (https://conda.io/)
